name: Spicy Turkish Ground Chicken Kabobs
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons red bell pepper, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler.
2. In a large bowl, combine the ground chicken, egg whites, onion, red bell pepper, and 2 tablespoons olive oil. Mix gently until well blended.
3. Divide into even portions equal to double the number of servings being made (e.g., if making for four, divide into 8 portions).
4. Form each portion into a sausage about 2 1/2" - 3" long.
5. Insert a flat-bladed skewer lengthwise through the meat and gently flatten it into an oval shape around the skewer (about 3" x 1 1/2").
6. Repeat, placing two kabobs on each skewer.
7. Arrange skewers on a foil-lined broiling tray. Brush lightly with remaining olive oil.
8. Broil about 4 inches from the heat source until done but not browned (about 1 1/2 - 2 minutes per side).
9. Serve immediately over rice pilaf.
Serving Ideas: Serve with cucumbers in yogurt and a rice pilaf.
